questions.q: Define alcohols and give their general formula.
questions.answer: Alcohols are organic compounds in which a hydroxyl (-OH) group is directly attached to a carbon atom. Their general formula is C_nH_(2n+1)OH.

questions.q: State the intermolecular forces present in alcohols.
questions.answer: Alcohols show hydrogen bonding due to the presence of hydroxyl (-OH) group.
Phenol is an aromatic alcohol in which the hydroxyl (-OH) group is attached to a benzene ring.

questions.q: State the general formula of ethers.
questions.answer: The general formula of ethers is R-O-R', where R and R' are alkyl groups.

questions.q: What is the effect of increasing the molecular mass of an alcohol on its boiling point?
questions.answer: Increasing the molecular mass of an alcohol increases its boiling point. This is because the intermolecular forces of attraction increase with an increase in molecular mass, which requires more energy for separation.

questions.q: Why does the boiling point of phenol exceed that of benzene?
questions.answer: The boiling point of phenol exceeds that of benzene due to the presence of intermolecular hydrogen bonding in phenol, which is not present in benzene.

questions.q: Give the structure of ethanol and draw its resonance structures.
questions.answer: The structure of ethanol is CH_(3)CH_(2)OH. It has two resonance structures: CH_(3)CH=O^- and CH_(3)CH-O^-, which show the delocalization of the negative charge.

questions.q: Describe the preparation of phenol from cumene.
questions.answer: Phenol is prepared from cumene by the reaction of cumene with air and a catalyst, followed by acid treatment to give phenol and acetone.

questions.q: What is the characteristic reaction of phenol?
questions.answer: The characteristic reaction of phenol is its ability to form a phenoxide ion when treated with a strong base.

questions.q: Give the structure of diethyl ether and draw its resonance structures.
questions.answer: The structure of diethyl ether is CH_(3)CH_(2)OCH_(2)CH_(3). It has two resonance structures: CH_(3)CH=O^- and CH_(3)CH-O^-, which show the delocalization of the negative charge.

questions.q: Describe the preparation of diethyl ether by the Williamson synthesis.
questions.answer: Diethyl ether is prepared by the Williamson synthesis by treating sodium ethoxide with bromoethane, followed by acid treatment to give diethyl ether and sodium bromide.

questions.q: What is the effect of increasing the molecular mass of an ether on its boiling point?
questions.answer: Increasing the molecular mass of an ether increases its boiling point. This is because the intermolecular forces of attraction increase with an increase in molecular mass, which requires more energy for separation.

questions.q: Why does the boiling point of an ether exceed that of an alcohol of similar molecular mass?
questions.answer: The boiling point of an ether exceeds that of an alcohol of similar molecular mass because the intermolecular forces of attraction in an ether are weaker than those in an alcohol.

questions.q: Describe the reactions of alcohols with sodium and hydrogen peroxide.
questions.answer: Alcohols react with sodium to give alkoxides, while with hydrogen peroxide, they give alkyl hydroperoxides.

questions.q: What is the characteristic reaction of phenol with sodium?
questions.answer: The characteristic reaction of phenol with sodium is the formation of a sodium phenoxide.

questions.q: Describe the reaction of an ether with a strong acid.
questions.answer: An ether reacts with a strong acid to give an alkyl halide and water.
